sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, date_str VARCHAR(20), some_date DATE);
SELECT id, sys.str_to_date(date_str, '%m/%d/%Y') AS parsed_date FROM test_dates WHERE sys.str_to_date(date_str, '%m/%d/%Y') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, date '2021-12-15' AS selected_date FROM test_dates WHERE date_str = '' OR date_str IS NULL;
SELECT id, date '2019-01-01' + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE date_str IS NOT NULL) AS future_date FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, sys.str_to_date(date_str, '%m/%d/%Y') AS parsed_date FROM test_dates GROUP BY parsed_date HAVING parsed_date IS NOT NULL ORDER BY parsed_date DESC;
CREATE TABLE another_table (date_col DATE, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO another_table (date_col, description) VALUES (DATE '2020-05-01', 'Test date'), (DATE '2020-06-01', NULL);

SELECT t.id, a.description, DATE '2020-01-01' + INTERVAL 1 DAY * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table WHERE description IS NOT NULL) AS adjusted_date FROM test_dates t JOIN another_table a ON sys.str_to_date(t.date_str, '%m/%d/%Y') = a.date_col;
SELECT t.date_str, sys.str_to_date(t.date_str, '%m/%d/%Y') AS parsed_date, date '2020-02-29' - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates) AS boundary_check FROM test_dates t WHERE parsed_date IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE another_table SET date_col = date '2020-07-01' WHERE description = 'Test date' AND date_col IS NOT NULL;
